title: Flaky test failure under parallelism: native tree-sitter module resolution race on Windows
symptom: g3.test.ts fails intermittently when run with other tests in parallel; error is 'No native build was found for platform=win32 runtime=electron abi=137' in nativeTreeSitter.ts:58 (copyNativeBinding). Test passes in isolation and passes on rerun.
root_cause: Race condition during parallel test execution on Windows: removeStaleCopies() in one test process deletes temporary native module copies while peer processes are still resolving/loading them. Amplified by multiple native packages (tree-sitter, tree-sitter-python) increasing copy traffic, and ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 in VS Code terminals. [Uncertainty: exact race sequence and whether recent synthesize.ts diff contributed to changed test parallelism pattern unknown.]

lineage.detected: g3.test.ts: CLI and read-only client-neutral MCP expose the identical fail-closed G3 receipt

evidence: test:g3.test.ts: CLI and read-only client-neutral MCP expose the identical fail-closed G3 receipt
